title: Senior Consultant Data & Analytics - Projektmanagement (all genders)
required_skills: Data & Analytics architecture knowledge (Data Warehouse, Data Lake, Data Lakehouse)
bonus_skills: Practical consulting experience (idealerweise Praxiserfahrung im Consulting)
matched_skills: Project leadership / coordination — HSBC PMO (USD 8M digital transformation), Hapag-Lloyd sprint coordination
Cross-functional stakeholder coordination — 6 workstreams, Product/Engineering/Marketing/C-suite interface
Consulting & strategy — GTM strategy (IPC, GRG Banking), B2B product launches, executive advisory
Presales-adjacent experience — B2B GTM for 4+ product launches, trade fair client engagement (100+ B2B clients)
C-level reporting & data storytelling — executive investment decisions, burn-rate reports, C-suite escalation
missing_skills: Data & Analytics architecture (Data Warehouse, Data Lake, Data Lakehouse) — not present in CV
Cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, Google Cloud) — not mentioned anywhere in CV
AI tools competency — no AI/KI experience or tooling referenced
German language proficiency at 'very good' level — CV states B1 (intermediate), JD requires sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse
IT/Technology consulting background — CV experience is in banking/logistics PMO, not IT consulting
core_responsibilities: Scope definition and expectation management with clients and project teams
Leading and coordinating Data & Analytics projects as primary client contact
Team leadership and active contribution to consulting strategy
Collaborative data analysis with Data Architects, Engineers, and Scientists
Presales activities, tender management, and pitch preparation
recommended_emphasis: Highlight the USD 8M budget governance and multi-workstream PMO at HSBC as evidence of large-scale project leadership, as this is the closest match to the 'erprobte Projektleitungskompetenz' requirement.

Frame the cross-functional coordination role (Product/Engineering/Marketing/C-suite) at HSBC as stakeholder moderation in a complex technical environment to partially offset the lack of formal IT consulting background.
Leverage GTM strategy work at IPC and GRG Banking to demonstrate consulting and strategy capability, even if not in a Data & Analytics context.
Acknowledge the German language gap proactively — consider noting active improvement or willingness to advance to B2 / C1, as the JD explicitly requires 'sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse'.

Do not fabricate Data & Analytics technical skills — instead, position as a strong project governance professional who can bridge business and technical teams, and emphasize the ability to quickly learn new technology domains (supported by tech stack lifecycle management experience).
summary: This role is a weak match (45.7%) primarily due to critical gaps in technical domain knowledge: the JD requires deep familiarity with Data & Analytics architectures (Data Warehouse, Data Lake, Data Lakehouse), cloud platforms (Azure/AWS/GCP), and AI tools — none of which appear in the candidate's CV. Additionally, the role requires senior-level German proficiency (sehr gut), whereas the candidate holds only a B1 level. The candidate's genuine strengths — multi-workstream PMO governance, C-level stakeholder management, cross-functional coordination, and strategy support — are relevant to the project management and consulting dimensions of the role, but insufficient to offset the technical and language gaps for a 'Senior Consultant Data & Analytics' position at a leading IT consultancy. This application carries meaningful risk of early rejection; the candidate should consider whether to invest effort here or focus on PMO/governance-focused roles where the technical Data & Analytics background is not a primary requirement.
